args.report:
FIRST AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT

ATTORNEY REVIEW REQUIRED; NO LEGAL ADVICE

This draft amendment is a general template based only on the limited facts provided. It does not account for jurisdiction‑specific requirements (including any anti‑indemnity, prompt‑payment, retainage, prevailing wage, or other mandatory rules), any NNN/operating‑expense allocation matrix, or any flow‑down obligations in related contracts. It should be reviewed, completed, and customized by a licensed attorney in the relevant jurisdiction before use or signature.

---

This FIRST AMENDMENT TO COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (this "Amendment") is made as of [TBD] (the "Effective Date"), by and between [TBD], a [TBD] ("Landlord"), and [TBD], a [TBD] ("Tenant"). Landlord and Tenant are sometimes referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

RECITALS

A. Landlord and Tenant are parties to that certain Commercial Lease Agreement dated as of [TBD] (the "Base Lease"), concerning certain premises commonly known as [TBD] and more particularly described in the Base Lease (the "Premises").

B. The Base Lease may have been amended, supplemented, or modified by that certain [TBD, e.g., "First Amendment to Commercial Lease dated [TBD]"] (collectively with the Base Lease, the "Lease"). If there are no prior amendments, references in this Amendment to the "Lease" shall mean the Base Lease only.

C. The Parties desire to amend the Lease solely to modify the Monthly Base Rent payable by Tenant, and to confirm that, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, all other terms and conditions of the Lease remain unchanged and in full force and effect.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained in this Amendment and in the Lease, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, Landlord and Tenant agree as follows:

1. Defined Terms; References.
   1.1 Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Amendment shall have the meanings given to them in the Lease.
   1.2 All references in the Lease to "this Lease," "hereunder," "herein," "hereof," and words of similar import shall, from and after the Effective Date, be deemed to refer to the Lease as amended by this Amendment.

2. Modification of Monthly Base Rent Only.
   2.1 Replacement of Monthly Base Rent. Effective as of [TBD, e.g., "[TBD] 1, 20[TBD]" or "the first day of the first full calendar month following the Effective Date"] (the "Rent Adjustment Date"), the Monthly Base Rent payable by Tenant under the Lease for the Premises shall be modified and restated in its entirety as follows, and any prior schedule or table of Monthly Base Rent in the Lease is hereby deleted and replaced with the following:

   (a) For the period from the Rent Adjustment Date through and including [TBD], Monthly Base Rent shall be [TBD] Dollars ($[TBD]) per month.

   (b) For the period from [TBD] through and including [TBD], Monthly Base Rent shall be [TBD] Dollars ($[TBD]) per month.

   (c) For the period from [TBD] through and including [TBD], Monthly Base Rent shall be [TBD] Dollars ($[TBD]) per month.

   (d) If the Parties intend only a single new Monthly Base Rent amount with no further scheduled changes, then, in lieu of subsections (a)–(c) above, the following shall apply (and subsections (a)–(c) shall be deemed deleted):

       "Commencing on the Rent Adjustment Date and continuing through the remainder of the Term (as it may be extended or earlier terminated in accordance with the Lease), Monthly Base Rent shall be [TBD] Dollars ($[TBD]) per month."

   The Parties shall complete, select, and/or delete the alternative provisions above as appropriate for their agreed rent structure.

   2.2 No Change to Other Rent Components. Except for the modification to Monthly Base Rent expressly set forth in Section 2.1 above, all other monetary obligations of Tenant under the Lease, including without limitation:

   (a) additional rent, operating expenses, common area maintenance (CAM) charges, insurance, taxes, and any other pass‑through or NNN charges;

   (b) late charges, interest, and other fees or charges; and

   (c) any percentage rent or other rent components (if applicable),

   shall remain unchanged and shall continue to be calculated, billed, and paid in accordance with the terms of the Lease. This Amendment does not alter the existing allocation of responsibility for operating expenses, taxes, insurance, maintenance, repairs (including structural vs. non‑structural), or capital expenditures under the Lease; any NNN or expense‑sharing matrix or schedule in the Lease remains in effect without modification.

   2.3 No Waiver of Other Sums Due. Nothing in this Amendment shall be construed as a waiver or release of any amounts due and owing under the Lease prior to the Effective Date, including any unpaid rent, additional rent, or other charges, all of which shall remain payable in accordance with the Lease.

3. Ratification; No Other Changes.
   3.1 Ratification of Lease. Except as expressly modified by this Amendment, the Lease is hereby ratified and confirmed in all respects and shall remain in full force and effect. The Parties acknowledge and agree that the Lease, as amended by this Amendment, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter thereof and hereof.

   3.2 No Other Amendments. There are no oral or written agreements between Landlord and Tenant with respect to the Lease or the Premises other than the Lease and this Amendment. This Amendment is limited to the subject matter expressly set forth herein (modification of Monthly Base Rent) and shall not be construed to amend, modify, or waive any other term, covenant, or condition of the Lease.

   3.3 Conflicts. In the event of any conflict between the terms of this Amendment and the terms of the Lease, the terms of this Amendment shall control with respect to the subject matter hereof, and the conflicting provisions of the Lease shall be deemed modified only to the extent necessary to give effect to this Amendment.

4. Representations; Authority.
   4.1 Each Party represents and warrants to the other that (a) it has full power and authority to enter into this Amendment and to perform its obligations hereunder, (b) the person(s) signing this Amendment on its behalf are duly authorized to do so, and (c) this Amendment constitutes a valid and binding obligation of such Party, enforceable in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, and similar laws affecting creditors' rights generally and general principles of equity.

   4.2 Tenant acknowledges that Landlord has not provided, and is not providing, any legal, tax, accounting, or other professional advice in connection with this Amendment or the Lease, and that Tenant has been advised to consult with its own independent legal counsel and other advisors regarding this Amendment, the Lease, and the transactions contemplated hereby.

5. Miscellaneous.
   5.1 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Amendment may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Signatures delivered by facsimile, email (in .pdf or similar format), or other electronic means of transmission shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.

   5.2 Governing Law. The Lease's existing governing law provision shall continue to apply to this Amendment. If the Lease does not contain a governing law provision, this Amendment shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [TBD governing law state], without regard to its conflicts‑of‑law principles.

   5.3 Further Assurances. Each Party agrees to execute and deliver such further documents and to take such further actions as may be reasonably necessary or appropriate to carry out the intent and purposes of this Amendment.

   5.4 No Third‑Party Beneficiaries. This Amendment is made and entered into solely for the benefit of Landlord and Tenant and their respective permitted successors and assigns, and no other person or entity shall be deemed to be a third‑party beneficiary of this Amendment.

   5.5 Jurisdiction‑Specific Compliance. The Parties acknowledge that (a) this Amendment does not alter any indemnity, insurance, or risk‑allocation provisions in the Lease, and no change to such provisions is intended or made hereby; (b) any jurisdiction‑specific requirements relating to anti‑indemnity statutes, prompt‑payment or retainage rules, or prevailing wage laws (to the extent applicable to the Lease or the underlying project) are not modified by this Amendment and shall continue to apply as set forth in the Lease or applicable law; and (c) this Amendment does not create or modify any flow‑down obligations to or from any subcontractors, subtenants, or other third parties, and any such obligations shall continue to be governed solely by the Lease and any separate agreements with such parties.

6. Attorney‑Review / No‑Legal‑Advice Disclaimer.

   THIS AMENDMENT HAS BEEN PREPARED AS A TEMPLATE BASED SOLELY ON LIMITED INFORMATION PROVIDED AND IS NOT TAILORED TO ANY SPECIFIC TRANSACTION, PROPERTY, OR JURISDICTION. IT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE LEGAL, TAX, OR OTHER PROFESSIONAL ADVICE. LANDLORD AND TENANT ARE EACH STRONGLY ADVISED TO CONSULT WITH THEIR OWN INDEPENDENT LEGAL COUNSEL LICENSED IN THE RELEVANT JURISDICTION, WHO CAN REVIEW THE LEASE, THIS AMENDMENT, ANY RELATED CONTRACTS (INCLUDING ANY SUBLEASES OR SUBCONTRACTS), AND ALL APPLICABLE LAWS (INCLUDING ANY ANTI‑INDEMNITY, PROMPT‑PAYMENT, RETAINAGE, PREVAILING WAGE, OR SIMILAR STATUTES), AND CAN CONFIRM THAT ANY NNN/OPERATING‑EXPENSE ALLOCATION MATRIX AND ANY FLOW‑DOWN OBLIGATIONS ARE APPROPRIATELY ADDRESSED BEFORE THIS AMENDMENT IS EXECUTED OR RELIED UPON.

[Signature page follows]

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Amendment as of the Effective Date.
